sourceMovie.title: Masterminds
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Masterminds (2016) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: 2 Guns
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Masterminds clicked for you, 2 Guns (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Masterminds?
faq.answer: Try 2 Guns, Beverly Hills Cop II, Mad Money—each shares DNA with Masterminds while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Masterminds?
faq.answer: Masterminds (2016) is best described as Action, Comedy, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Masterminds worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
